title: rhynchospora ceylanica
intro: Rhynchospora ceylanica, also known as the Sri Lankan club-rush, is a plant species belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. Rhynchospora ceylanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal use.
